expectations.text: The agent searches the repo for existing test files to check whether the proposed naming rule holds
expectations.evidence: Agent used Glob to list all tests/**/*.py files under /Users/greg/code/agent-skills, explicitly to check whether existing test files already follow the proposed skill-prefixed naming convention before deciding whether to adopt the rule.

expectations.text: The agent identifies at least two existing test files that do not follow the proposed skill-prefixed naming rule
expectations.evidence: Agent identified multiple counter-examples including tests/js-deps/test_argument_parsing.py, tests/js-deps/test_workflow_routing.py, tests/pr-comments/test_comment_classification.py, tests/learn/test_detection.py, and tests/learn/test_edge_cases.py — all lacking a skill-prefixed basename.

expectations.text: The agent does not adopt the 'all test files must be skill-prefixed' rule verbatim into CLAUDE.md without modification
expectations.evidence: Agent explicitly declined to update CLAUDE.md with the stricter 'must' rule, noting it would retroactively make most of the test suite non-compliant. No edit to CLAUDE.md was made adopting the verbatim 'all test files must follow this pattern' language.

expectations.text: The agent either softens the rule to a preference (changes 'must' to 'prefer' or similar) or declines the suggestion, and references the counter-examples found in its plan note or reply
expectations.evidence: Agent declined the suggestion entirely, composing a reply that explicitly cites counter-examples (test_argument_parsing.py, test_workflow_routing.py, test_comment_classification.py, test_detection.py, test_edge_cases.py) and explains that the existing CLAUDE.md rule already uses 'prefer' rather than 'must', which is the appropriate level of guidance given the mixed existing convention.
